items.title: Optimizer and learning-rate tuning
items.description: Diagnosing plateaus, cliffs, and oscillations on the loss surface guides learning-rate schedules, momentum, and adaptive optimizers when training stalls or diverges.

items.title: Initialization strategy
items.description: Different starting points on a non-convex surface lead to different basins; Xavier/He init and multiple seeds are landscape-aware choices.

items.title: Generalization and flat minima
items.description: Comparing valley sharpness (loss under small weight perturbations) helps explain why some low-loss solutions generalize and others overfit.

items.title: Toy-model teaching and research
items.description: Low-dimensional slices of L(θ) are used in papers and courses to illustrate saddles, mode connectivity, and the effect of batch noise on effective terrain.
